concerns.text: Task T025 defines straight-lining detection as 'Calculate variance of the multi-item scale scores (one score per post) across ALL 5 posts'. This contradicts spec FR-003 and Edge Case 2, which define straight-lining as 'zero variance across 7 items' (the scale items themselves). The task implements a variance check across posts (n=5) rather than across scale items (n=7), failing to satisfy the specific data quality constraint mandated by the spec.

Task T051 mandates a 'hard pipeline failure' if the stratification difference exceeds a threshold (e.g., 0.05). The spec (US-1 Acceptance Scenario 3 and SC-006) only requires 'reporting' the difference to verify validity. There is no requirement in the spec to halt execution if stratification is imperfect. This task introduces an unauthorized blocking constraint that violates the spec's acceptance criteria.

Tasks T041 and T042 implement logic to 'Raise an error' if memory/runtime thresholds are exceeded. The spec (FR-007, SC-005) defines these as performance goals ('ensure all analysis operations complete within... constraints') and feasibility tests, not as runtime error-throwing mechanisms that abort the research pipeline. This task relaxes the spec's intent by converting a feasibility check into a hard failure condition not present in the requirements.

Tasks T033 and T035 implement a mandatory switch to a Linear Mixed-Effects (LME) model if ICC >= 0.05. While the Plan.md supports this, the Spec (FR-005) strictly mandates: 'System MUST perform an independent-samples t-test'. The spec does not authorize switching statistical models based on ICC. This task silently relaxes the strict requirement of FR-005, creating a deliverable that does not match the specified acceptance criteria (t-test output).
